name: solidity-security
description: Defensive Solidity coding patterns. 13-point pre-deploy checklist. Covers reentrancy, token decimals, SafeERC20, oracle safety, MEV protection, proxy patterns, EIP-712, delegatecall, and automated tooling (Slither, Mythril, Foundry fuzzing).

capabilities.reentrancy_defense: CEI pattern + ReentrancyGuard on all external-calling functions
capabilities.token_decimal_handling: Never hardcode 1e18 — check decimals(), normalize across tokens
capabilities.safe_erc20: Always SafeERC20 — handles USDT, fee-on-transfer, rebasing, pausable
capabilities.oracle_safety: Chainlink with staleness+sanity checks, TWAP 30min+, never DEX spot
capabilities.vault_inflation: Virtual offset defense for ERC-4626 first-depositor attack
capabilities.mev_protection: Flashbots Protect RPC, tight slippage, MEV-aware DEX aggregators
capabilities.proxy_safety: UUPS recommended, storage append-only, initializer pattern, multisig admin
capabilities.eip712_signatures: Domain separation, nonce, deadline, OpenZeppelin ERC20Permit
capabilities.delegatecall_safety: Never delegatecall to user-supplied address, match storage layouts
capabilities.access_control: Explicit restrictions on every state-changing function, role-based
capabilities.input_validation: Zero address, zero amount, bounds, array length checks
capabilities.static_analysis: Slither + Mythril before deploy, never ignore high/medium findings

pre_deploy_checklist: Access control on every admin function
Return values: SafeERC20 for all token ops
Input validation: zero address, zero amount, bounds
Events emitted on every state change
Edge cases tested: zero, max, unauthorized, reentrancy
rules: This skill is for DEFENSIVE coding — use evm-audit for auditing others' contracts
Pre-deploy checklist is mandatory before ANY contract deployment
Always use OpenZeppelin contracts as base — battle-tested and audited
Multiply before divide — division first = precision loss
Pausable + onlyOwner = censorship risk — flag and suggest timelocks/multisig
